assertionResults.evidence: test_awq_dual_gemv.py: All numerical equivalence tests PASSED. max_abs_err=0.000209-0.000246 < 1e-2 threshold. Tested shapes: N=11008/13696/17408, K=5120, group_size=128.
assertionResults.consoleOutput:
VAL-AWQ-DUAL-001: ALL equivalence tests PASSED
  ✓ N=11008, K= 5120: max_abs_err=2.094209e-04
  ✓ N=13696, K= 5120: max_abs_err=2.033412e-04
  ✓ N=17408, K= 5120: max_abs_err=2.456307e-04

assertionResults.evidence: test_awq_dual_dispatch.py failed due to test design issue: creates minimal engine without loading weights, so build_dispatch_cache() returns empty layer cache. The kernel selection logic exists in engine.py (lines 3455-3477) and correctly selects AWQ dual kernel (12 params) vs GPTQ dual kernel (14 params) when _awq_mode=True and _gemv_int4_dual_awq_fused is available. Manual code inspection confirms correct implementation.

metrics.test_design_issue: No weights loaded, no layer cache built

assertionResults.consoleOutput: Test logs show: 'GEMV INT4 dual AWQ (fused gate+up+silu, no zero-point) loaded' and 'AWQ GEMV mode enabled: using gemv_int4_v5_awq_t16 + gemv_int4_dual_awq_fused (no zero-point)' indicating kernel is loaded and AWQ mode is active.

assertionResults.evidence: test_awq_dual_gemv.py: All throughput benchmarks PASSED. AWQ dual kernel shows 1.034-1.058x speedup over GPTQ dual kernel, exceeding 1.02x target. Zero-point arithmetic savings (8 v_sub_f32 per uint32) translate to measurable throughput improvement.
assertionResults.consoleOutput:
Throughput Report:
  ✓ N=11008, K= 5120: GPTQ=144.2us, AWQ=139.5us, speedup=1.034x
  ✓ N=13696, K= 5120: GPTQ=169.2us, AWQ=163.1us, speedup=1.037x
  ✓ N=17408, K= 5120: GPTQ=205.9us, AWQ=194.7us, speedup=1.058x

assertionResults.evidence: E2E test blocked: AWQ model at /opt/models/Qwen3.5-27B-AWQ is detected as 'gptq' format (missing AWQ-specific file markers). Running AWQ kernel mode with GPTQ weights causes NaN/Inf because AWQ kernel skips zero-point subtraction (assumes zeros=0), but GPTQ weights have non-zero zero points. C dispatch integration code exists (engine.py lines 3455-3477) but cannot be validated E2E without true AWQ model weights.
assertionResults.consoleOutput:
Detecting formats:
  AWQ model format: gptq
  GPTQ model format: gptq

With AWQ mode enabled on GPTQ weights:
  'Step 0: NaN/Inf detected in output!' for all steps.

Kernel loading confirms AWQ dual available:
  'GEMV INT4 dual AWQ (fused gate+up+silu, no zero-point) loaded'

frictions.description: test_awq_dual_dispatch.py creates engine without weights, so layer cache is empty and ffn_gate_up_silu not found
frictions.suggestedFix: Test should either use mock weights or skip layer cache validation when weights not loaded

frictions.description: AWQ model at /opt/models/Qwen3.5-27B-AWQ lacks AWQ format markers - detected as GPTQ
frictions.suggestedFix: Need true AWQ model (with qweight, qzeros=0, scales) to validate E2E

blockers.description: AWQ model directory exists but detect_awq_format() returns 'gptq' - model may not be in AWQ format or missing format markers
